iti28:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti28:2.1: “Endowed with two things, monks, a monk lives in stress in the present life–troubled, distressed,
iti28:2.2: & feverish–and at the break-up of the body, after death, a bad destination can be expected.
iti28:2.4: A lack of guarding of the doors of the sense faculties, and knowing no moderation in food.
iti28:2.5: Endowed with these two things, a monk lives in stress in the present life–troubled, distressed,
iti28:2.6: & feverish–and at the break-up of the body, after death, a bad destination can be expected.”
iti28:3.3: when a monk leaves these doors unguarded
iti28:4.4: stress in body, stress in mind.
iti28:5.3: whether by day or by night,
iti28:5.4: one like this lives in suffering & stress.
